# n-dx

> Codebase analysis generated by Sourcevision.
> When using this document: group related items together and separate unrelated concerns.

## Project Overview

- **Files**: 1007
- **Lines of code**: 337,024
- **Languages**: TypeScript (845), CSS (44), JSON (30), Markdown (30), JavaScript (29)
- **Zones**: 22 (22 with descriptions)
- **Git**: feature/integrate-codex @ 21786d2
- **Analyzed**: 2026-03-03T16:00:16.216Z

## Route Structure

### Server API Routes

**/api/hench/adaptive/** (handleAdaptiveRoute) — `packages/web/src/server/routes-adaptive.ts`

```
DELETE  /api/hench/adaptive/override/:key
GET     /api/hench/adaptive/analysis
GET     /api/hench/adaptive/history
GET     /api/hench/adaptive/settings
POST    /api/hench/adaptive/apply
POST    /api/hench/adaptive/dismiss/:id
POST    /api/hench/adaptive/lock/:key
POST    /api/hench/adaptive/override
POST    /api/hench/adaptive/settings
POST    /api/hench/adaptive/unlock/:key
```

**/api/ndx-config** (handleConfigRoute) — `packages/web/src/server/routes-config.ts`

```
GET     /api/ndx-config
GET     /api/projects
POST    /api/projects/switch
```

**/api/features** (handleFeaturesRoute) — `packages/web/src/server/routes-features.ts`

```
GET     /api/features
PUT     /api/features
```

**/api/hench/** (handleHenchRoute) — `packages/web/src/server/routes-hench.ts`

```
DELETE  /api/hench/templates/:id
GET     /api/hench/audit

GET     /api/hench/memory/history/:taskId

POST    /api/hench/execute/:taskId/terminate
```

### Components: 5 total, 0 usage edges

## Findings

### Anti-patterns

- All workspace package dependencies resolve as 'external' in the static import graph, causing zone coupling scores to structurally underreport real cross-package dependency depth for every zone. The coupling metric is blind to intended workspace consumption, making it impossible to use sourcevision's coupling scores to reason about compliance with the four-tier dependency hierarchy — a separate cross-package dependency audit tool or lint rule is required for that purpose. [warning]
- No automated import boundary enforcement exists (no ESLint import-no-restricted-paths rules, no dependency-cruiser configuration, no CI gate). The four-tier dependency hierarchy and gateway pattern are enforced entirely by convention — a developer adding a direct cross-tier import in any leaf file will bypass the architecture with no automated rejection and the violation will only surface during code review if a reviewer recognizes it. [warning]
- The absence of a shared types package forces independent type definition across domain packages. Types that conceptually span rex and hench (task IDs, status enums, tree node shapes) are defined in rex and consumed by hench via gateway re-exports — if rex ever restructures its type definitions, hench and web must both update their gateway imports simultaneously. A dedicated @n-dx/types foundation package would decouple type evolution from domain logic changes. [info]
- The canonical messaging pipeline wiring (throttle → coalescer → rateLimiter → dedup) exists only in test infrastructure. Any production consumer that re-implements this wiring (e.g. a second viewer or a server-side handler) must rediscover the correct composition order from the test file. Extracting a createMessagingPipeline factory to production code would make the pipeline order explicit, testable as a unit, and prevent divergence. [warning]
- The messaging directory lacks an index.ts despite containing four cohesive utilities with uniform API shapes. The absence of a barrel export is the root cause of the three-zone fragmentation, the 7 cross-zone import edges, and the below-threshold cohesion scores in viewer-message-flow-control and viewer-request-deduplication. Adding messaging/index.ts is the minimum viable fix that resolves all three symptoms simultaneously. [warning]
- The web package has no enforced boundary between its Node.js server layer (src/server/) and its browser client layer (src/viewer/). Both are in the same zone with the same import rules. A tsconfig path alias, package.json exports condition, or lint rule scoped to each subdirectory would make cross-environment imports a compile-time or lint error rather than a silent runtime failure. [warning]
- Three production files across two zones (lazy-children.ts, listener-lifecycle.ts, node-culler.ts) have no production importers anywhere in the codebase — confirmed by full import graph traversal. If dynamic import() is not in use, these files are dead code kept alive only by unit tests. A build-output audit (checking whether these modules appear in any compiled bundle) would definitively resolve the ambiguity and either confirm dead-code removal candidates or document the dynamic import contract. [warning]
- God function: handleHenchRoute in packages/web/src/server/routes-hench.ts calls 59 unique functions — consider decomposing into smaller, focused functions [warning]

### Patterns

- All 5 circular dependency cycles are confined to two packages: rex/src/analyze/ (3 cycles among reason.ts, extract.ts, file-validation.ts) and sourcevision/src/analyzers/ (2 cycles between enrich.ts and zones.ts). No cross-package circular dependencies exist, confirming the gateway pattern successfully prevents inter-package cycles — but intra-package cycle hygiene is an open issue. [info]
- All 5 circular dependency cycles are confined within individual packages (rex analyze subsystem; sourcevision analyzers subsystem) — no inter-zone cycles exist, confirming the zone boundary map is architecturally cycle-free at the macro level. [info]
- All 5 detected circular dependency chains are likely intra-zone given the clean inter-zone import graph — they increase incremental compilation cost and mask true module boundaries within the 330-file hub, and should be resolved by introducing intermediate abstractions or reordering initialization dependencies. [warning]
- web-5 and web-6 are the only inter-zone coupling bottlenecks; both receive imports from exactly two consumers (web-viewer and web-integration). Formalizing them as named shared-utilities modules with documented public APIs would make their structural role explicit and prevent further ad-hoc imports from additional consumers. [info]
- web-integration sits between web-viewer and the messaging primitives (web-5, web-6) in the import graph but does not serve as a true facade — web-viewer bypasses it with 6 direct imports to the primitives while also holding 1 import to web-integration. web-integration is a partially-realized abstraction layer: present in the graph but not structurally enforced. [warning]
- CONTRADICTION RESOLUTION: Global findings 14 and 16 are misdiagnoses caused by zone naming inconsistency. Finding 16 characterizes the web-viewer → web-integration import as a 'test-layer isolation violation'; finding 14 characterizes web-integration as a 'partially-realized abstraction layer'. Both conclusions depend on web-integration being an integration-test zone. Finding 33 establishes it is viewer-call-rate-limiter, a production messaging utility. The import is a valid production-to-production dependency. Both findings have been downgraded to info. No remediation is warranted. [info]

### Relationships

- Hub-and-satellite topology: web-dashboard-mcp-server is the sole integration hub while web-landing-page, prd-tree-lifecycle-tests, prd-tree-node-culler, and viewer-static-assets are zero-coupling satellites — a clean, scalable pattern where each satellite can be updated or replaced without hub changes. [info]
- Production-to-integration-test import (web-viewer → web-integration, 1 import): a production module imports a symbol from the integration-test zone, violating test-layer isolation — the shared symbol should be moved to a non-test utilities module. [warning]
- The web messaging subsystem (3 zones, 7 cross-zone import edges) is the only part of the codebase where coupling scores exceed 0.5 and cohesion scores fall below 0.5. Every other package boundary has cohesion ≥ 0.99 and coupling ≤ 0.01, making the messaging cluster an isolated anomaly rather than a systemic pattern. [warning]
- packages/rex/src/analyze/reason.ts participates in a circular dependency with extract.ts (detected twice) and a 3-node cycle with file-validation.ts — with 24 inbound importers, any attempt to break these cycles risks cascading type errors across a significant portion of the rex analyze subsystem. [warning]
- packages/sourcevision/src/analyzers/enrich.ts and zones.ts form a circular dependency detected twice — both files are in the critical 4-phase analyze pipeline, meaning the cycle directly affects the zone detection output that every downstream consumer (hench briefs, web dashboard, rex recommendations) depends on. [warning]
- packages/web/tests/unit/server/type-consistency.test.ts directly imports packages/rex/src/schema/v1.ts alongside the web rex-gateway, creating an undeclared compile-time dependency on rex schema internals from test code — if rex's schema module path changes the test breaks silently until CI runs, and the direct import is a gateway bypass even if intentional for type verification. [warning]
- Tightly coupled modules: packages/web/src/server/routes-rex.ts and packages/web/src/server/types.ts — 142 cross-file calls (unidirectional). Consider extracting shared interface or merging [info]

### Observations

- High cohesion (1) — files are tightly interconnected [info] (.rex)
- High cohesion (1) — files are tightly interconnected [info] (docs)
- High cohesion (1) — files are tightly interconnected [info] (e2e)
- 5 circular dependency chains detected — see imports.json for details [warning]
- Size imbalance: largest zone has 330 files vs smallest with 2 — uneven decomposition [info]
- All five core architectural zones (root, .rex, docs, hench, web) have cohesion 1.0 and coupling 0.0, confirming that package-level and data-directory boundaries are correctly enforced across the monorepo. [info]
- Five of the seven web zones in this batch have cohesion of 1.0 and coupling of 0 — the web package's internal design is exceptionally clean, with isolated leaves and a well-bounded core hub. [info]
- Integration test files placed in zones determined by import proximity rather than source ownership (e.g., request-dedup integration test in the rate-limiter zone) reduce the diagnostic value of cohesion scores — consider co-locating tests with their source files or establishing a test-placement convention. [warning]
- The hench package (159 files) and web-viewer (330 files) are the two largest zones by file count — their size relative to other zones suggests they may benefit from further internal modularization to support independent testing and reduce cognitive load for new contributors. [info]
- The messaging subsystem spans three zones with overlapping concerns; treating viewer-call-rate-limiter, viewer-message-flow-control, and viewer-request-deduplication as a single 'viewer/messaging' package boundary would eliminate cross-zone coupling warnings and simplify web-viewer's import surface. [info]
- ... and 13 more

### Suggestions

- @n-dx/llm-client's public.ts does not export DEFAULT_CODEX_MODEL or DEFAULT_MODEL, forcing three domain packages to independently hardcode the same string literals. Add these constants to llm-client's public API so the foundation layer is the single source of truth for default model names, consistent with the stated four-tier dependency contract where domain packages depend on foundation rather than duplicating its values. [warning]
- All five zero-importer production files (lazy-children.ts, listener-lifecycle.ts, node-culler.ts, use-dom-performance-monitor.ts, dom-performance-monitor.ts indirectly via the hook) are in the src/viewer/ subtree. Run a Vite bundle output audit targeting these five modules specifically to confirm whether they appear in any compiled chunk. Record the result as a comment in each file — either documenting the dynamic import() call site or marking the file as a dead-code removal candidate. [warning]
- Establish a CODEOWNERS file with entries for gateway files (src/server/rex-gateway.ts, src/server/domain-gateway.ts, src/prd/rex-gateway.ts), the four-tier boundary packages, and high-fanin files (packages/rex/src/core/tree.ts, packages/rex/src/schema/index.ts). These files are the highest-blast-radius targets for architectural drift and currently have no automated reviewer assignment. [info]
- Re-audit all 'warning' and 'critical' findings from enrichment passes 1–3 that reference 'web-integration' as an integration-test zone. The zone is a production messaging utility (viewer-call-rate-limiter). Findings that characterize the web-viewer → web-integration import as a layer violation should be removed or reclassified as correct production-to-production imports. [warning]
- Semantic zone naming must precede multi-pass architectural enrichment. The current zones.json uses meaningful IDs (viewer-call-rate-limiter, dom-performance-monitoring) while the enrichment context still references opaque aliases (web-integration, web-4). Re-run zone enrichment with the current semantic IDs as the baseline to eliminate accumulated misdiagnoses from earlier passes. [warning]
- The four messaging utilities share an identical factory-pattern API (createX, Config, Runtime) that is implicit and unenforced. Introducing a shared MessagingUtility<TConfig, TRuntime> generic interface would formalize the contract, enable TypeScript to catch non-conforming future additions, and document the intended pattern for the messaging directory. [info]
- The messaging directory exhibits a directory-to-zone mismatch unique in the codebase: four files, one directory, three zones. This is the structural fingerprint of a missing index.ts — the single root cause behind zone fragmentation, depressed cohesion scores, and web-viewer's inflated import surface. Adding packages/web/src/viewer/messaging/index.ts is the highest-leverage change available: it closes all three symptoms with one file. [warning]
- Three viewer subdirectories (messaging/, polling/, performance/) all lack index.ts barrel exports despite containing cohesive utilities consumed by 4, 11, and 12 files respectively. Adopting a monorepo-wide convention that every utility subdirectory with 3+ consumers exposes an index.ts would retroactively fix all three directories and prevent the pattern from recurring in new subdirectories. [warning]
- Zone naming is inconsistent across the viewer subsystem: 'dom-performance-monitoring' omits the 'viewer-' prefix applied to all three messaging zones. Adopting a convention where zone names derive from their source directory path (src/viewer/* → viewer-*) would make the zone map self-documenting and eliminate the current outlier. [info]
- Add a dependency-cruiser configuration at the monorepo root codifying the four-tier hierarchy as enforced rules: Orchestration may import Execution; Execution may import Domain; Domain packages may not import each other; Foundation may not import above its tier. Include workspace: true in the resolver config so workspace package references are resolved to their actual source paths rather than treated as external. This single config addition converts finding 23 (convention-only enforcement) from a standing risk into a CI gate, and addresses finding 22 (workspace deps invisible to coupling metrics) simultaneously. [warning]
- ... and 18 more

1. **2 zones exceed architectural risk thresholds (cohesion < 0.4, coupling > 0.6): …**

1. **All workspace package dependencies resolve as 'external' in… (+6 related)**
